components.desc: forge.py entrypoint honoring the contract `python3 forge.py build <src-dir> <out-dir> [--theme NAME]`. argparse-based; loads optional <src-dir>/site.json (json stdlib); resolves theme precedence --theme > site.json 'theme' > 'classic'; validates src-dir exists (nonzero exit + stderr message otherwise); creates out-dir; exposes a build(src, out, config) function that later components extend. Defines the internal page dict shape ({path, name, title, md, html}) as docstring so siblings integrate against it. check.sh: run build on tmp src with one dummy .md and a site.json, assert exit 0, out-dir created, bad src-dir gives nonzero exit; trap cleanup.

F1: stdlib-only markdown parser + HTML renderer module wired into forge.py build. Subset: ATX h1-h3, paragraphs, **bold**, *italic*, [text](url), inline `code`, fenced code blocks with optional lang tag (rendered as <pre><code class="lang-X">), unordered - lists. Every page.md -> page.html full document: <head> with <title> = first h1 else filename, <link> to theme CSS file (path only; CSS shipped by themes component, renderer just links style.css). ALL raw HTML in md escaped (html.escape) — md is untrusted. Edge cases: empty file -> valid empty page; unclosed fence -> rest of file is code, no crash. check.sh: build tmp fixture covering every construct + a <script> injection + empty file + unclosed fence; grep rendered HTML for expected tags, escaped script, exit 0; trap cleanup.

F2: after per-page render, scan all pages sorted by filename; generate index.html listing every page (title + link); if index.md exists its body renders above the page list. Inject <nav> into every rendered page (including index) with a link per page, current page marked class="active". Single-page site still gets nav + index. Integration contract: nav injection happens between render and plugin on_html so plugins see final page HTML; site dict {pages:[page...], config} passed downstream. check.sh: tmp 3-page fixture -> assert index.html lists all 3, every page contains nav with all links + exactly one active, plus a link-checker pass (parse every href in out-dir with html.parser, assert target file exists — zero dead links); single-page fixture also passes; trap cleanup.

F3: self-contained plugin subsystem in plugins/ + loader wired into forge.py. Loader reads site.json "plugins":[...], imports each plugins/<name>.py via importlib, runs hooks in list order: on_page(page)->dict pre-render, on_html(page, html)->str post-render(after nav injection), on_site(site, out_dir)->None post-build. Unknown plugin name -> clear stderr error + nonzero exit; plugin exception -> build fails with plugin name in message. Ships two plugins: highlight (on_html: token-level <span class="tok-*"> spans inside lang-tagged fenced blocks; python keywords/strings/comments/numbers + generic fallback strings/numbers; untagged blocks untouched; regex-based, no pygments) and rss (on_site: deterministic RSS 2.0 feed.xml at out root, one <item>/page with title/link/first-paragraph description, channel meta from site.json, no wall-clock dates, parses with xml.etree). Hook contract documented in plugins/README.md. "plugins":[] -> no tok- spans, no feed.xml.
components.sub_goal:
# Sub-PRD: site-forge plugin subsystem

## Context
Host pipeline exists: `python3 forge.py build <src> <out> [--theme NAME]` parses md -> renders HTML -> injects nav -> writes site. Page dict shape: {path, name, title, md, html}. Site dict: {pages, config}. This subtree builds the plugin layer only. python3 stdlib ONLY. Every component ships executable check.sh: non-interactive, own tmp dirs, trap cleanup, <60s, safe under make -j2.

## Components in scope

### Loader + hook API
- Plugin = python file in plugins/ exposing any of: `on_page(page: dict) -> dict` (mutate parsed page pre-render), `on_html(page: dict, html: str) -> str` (mutate final page HTML, runs after nav injection), `on_site(site: dict, out_dir: str) -> None` (post-build whole-site artifacts).
- Discovers plugins from site.json `"plugins": [...]`, loads via importlib, runs hooks in list order. Missing hook attrs are fine (all optional).
- Unknown plugin name -> clear error on stderr, nonzero exit. Plugin raising in any hook -> build fails loudly, plugin name in stderr, nonzero exit. No silent skips.
- Hook contract documented in plugins/README.md.
- check.sh: tmp fixture with a trivial test plugin proving each hook fires in order; unknown-name run exits nonzero with name in stderr; raising plugin run exits nonzero with name in stderr.

### Shipped plugin: highlight
- on_html: fenced code blocks WITH language tag get <span class="tok-*"> markup. Python: keywords, strings, comments, numbers (classes tok-kw, tok-str, tok-com, tok-num). Unknown languages: generic fallback highlighting strings + numbers. Blocks without lang tag pass through byte-identical.
- regex/tokenize stdlib only. Must not double-escape: operates on already-escaped code content.
- check.sh: fixture with python block, unknown-lang block, untagged block; assert tok-kw/tok-str/tok-com/tok-num present for python, tok-str/tok-num for unknown, untagged block unchanged, output still valid (no nested/broken spans via html.parser parse).

### Shipped plugin: rss
- on_site: write feed.xml (RSS 2.0) at out-dir root. One <item> per page: title, link, description = first paragraph text. Channel title/link/description from site.json keys. Deterministic: fixed or omitted pubDate, never wall clock — two consecutive builds produce identical bytes.
- check.sh: build tmp fixture, parse feed.xml with xml.etree.ElementTree, assert channel meta + item count == page count + descriptions non-empty; build twice, cmp feed.xml identical.

## Acceptance (subsystem)
- Both plugins enabled in one run: HTML contains tok- spans AND feed.xml exists.
- `"plugins": []`: zero tok- spans anywhere, no feed.xml.
- Broken plugin (raises): nonzero exit, plugin name in stderr.
- An integration check.sh at subsystem root runs all three scenarios.

components.desc: F4: two CSS themes shipped as local files copied into out-dir (no CDN): classic (white bg, dark text, serif headings) and midnight (#0b0e14 bg, light text, accent links/nav). Same HTML, CSS-only diff; selected via existing --theme/site.json resolution from cli-scaffold. Both style nav, h1-h3, body, links, lists, and tok-kw/tok-str/tok-com/tok-num spans. No animations, no wall-clock content. Screenshot eval: build fixture page (headings, list, links, nav, python code block highlighted via highlight plugin), for each theme run $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/evals/snap out/page.html shots/<theme>.png 480 640 then evalshot against committed goldens/<theme>.png. check.sh: build fixture with both themes into tmp out-dirs, snap + evalshot both; also assert CSS file exists in out-dir and page links it; trap cleanup.

F5 + top-level acceptance: docs-src/ with >=4 real md pages documenting site-forge (index.md: what it is; usage.md: CLI + site.json reference; plugin-api.md: hook contract; themes.md: both themes) each with real code samples; docs-src/site.json enabling BOTH plugins + a theme. check.sh: build docs-src into tmp out-dir; assert index.html + >=4 page htmls, nav on every page, tok- spans present, feed.xml parses via xml.etree with >=4 items, zero dead hrefs in out-dir. This built site is the repo demo fixture; trap cleanup.
